title: pilosella subcymigera subsp. pseudovistulina
intro: Pilosella subcymigera subsp. pseudovistulina is a rare plant species within the Pilosella genus, commonly found in mountainous regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. Herbal preparations of Pilosella subcymigera subsp. pseudovistulina are typically made from the plant's aerial parts and can be consumed as infusions, decoctions, or used as tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
